channel.title: NYT > U.S. News
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: As Ukraine War Escalates, Witkoff and Kushner Are Focused on Iran
item.description: Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner are pivotal players at a moment when the posts of U.S. ambassador to Moscow and Kyiv are both vacant.
item.dc:creator: Anton Troianovski
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: Steve Witkoff, a White House special envoy, has met with President Vladimir V. Putin of Russia seven times since President Trump returned to office.

item.title: MAGA Base Stays Quiet After Trump Reports Billions in Personal Gains
item.description: A new mandatory disclosure revealed that the president has earned $2.2 billion during the first year back in the White House.
item.dc:creator: Emily Davies
item.media:credit: Pete Marovich for The New York Times
item.media:description: Some supporters of President Trump described his earnings as a validation of the business acumen they have long admired in him.

item.title: Louisiana Attorney General Charged With Bullying New Orleans Officials
item.description: Liz Murrill, a Republican, was indicted on charges of malfeasance and intimidation after sending warning letters to the city’s Democratic leaders over actions they took.
item.dc:creator: Rick Rojas
item.media:description: Attorney General Liz Murrill had warned municipal officials of “the possible forfeiture of your own office.”

item.title: UPS Did Not Tell Inspectors to Check Failed Part in Louisville Crash
item.description: Inspectors were not instructed to check a bearing that they had been told was faulty, but the shipping company says Boeing, the plane’s manufacturer, is to blame.
item.dc:creator: Karoun Demirjian
item.media:description: An MD-11 cargo jet operated by UPS crashed shortly after takeoff at the Louisville Muhammad Ali International Airport in November.

item.title: Texas Governor Appoints Former Hard Right Critic to Key Post
item.description: Gov. Greg Abbott appointed a firebrand former opponent, Don Huffines, to be state comptroller, underscoring a G.O.P. push for unity ahead of the midterms.
item.dc:creator: J. David Goodman
item.media:credit: Tamir Kalifa for The New York Times
item.media:description: Don Huffines at a forum featuring gubernatorial candidates running to unseat Gov. Greg Abbott in 2022.

item.title: F.B.I. Assigns Scores of Analysts to Examine Election Records in Georgia
item.description: The expansion of an investigation into Fulton County’s election office reflects President Trump’s desire to prove his baseless claims that the 2020 election in Georgia was rigged.
item.dc:creator: Nick Corasaniti, Alan Feuer, Dustin Volz and Richard Fausset
item.media:credit: Salwan Georges for The New York Times
item.media:description: A memo says that the surge is part of a priority effort by Kash Patel, the director of the F.B.I.

item.title: Judge Demands Answers About Plans for Trump’s East Potomac Golf Course
item.description: In a fiery hearing, Judge Ana C. Reyes hammered the government over denials that the president was forging ahead with plans to renovate the course without approval.
item.dc:creator: Zach Montague
item.category: White House East Wing and Ballroom (Washington, DC)
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump inspected plans for his proposed renovation of the East Potomac Golf Course on Sunday.

item.title: Tesla Driver Using Autopilot in Texas Crash Is Charged With Manslaughter
item.description: A front-door video camera of the crash, which killed a woman inside her home, showed the Tesla plowing into a house through its driveway.
item.dc:creator: Alexandra E. Petri
item.media:description: A Tesla Model 3 using the Full Self Driving Beta software in 2023.

item.title: JD Vance and Melania Trump Also Had Sharp Earnings Increases in 2025
item.description: But the revenue brought in by the vice president and the first lady paled in comparison to that of President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Andrea Fuller
item.media:credit: Salwan Georges for The New York Times; Allison Robbert for The New York Times
item.media:description: First Lady Melania Trump and Vice President JD Vance

item.title: Supreme Court Allows Reporter to Be Fined for Failing to Disclose Source
item.description: Catherine Herridge, a former Fox News reporter, was held in civil contempt by a lower court after she refused to reveal her sources for articles she wrote about a scientist who was investigated by the F.B.I.
item.dc:creator: Ann E. Marimow
item.category: Reporters Committee for Freedom of the Press
item.media:description: Catherine Herridge invoked First Amendment protections to try to avoid being forced to testify and identify her sources.

item.title: Jury Deadlocks on Felony Count for Golden Gate Bridge Protesters
item.description: Seven activists shut down the bridge in 2024 to protest American-backed military attacks in Gaza. They avoided a conviction on the most serious charge but were found guilty of misdemeanors.
item.dc:creator: Soumya Karlamangla
item.media:credit: Minh Connors for The New York Times
item.media:description: Supporters have shown up regularly at the San Francisco Superior Courthouse to back activists facing multiple charges for blocking Golden Gate Bridge in 2024.

item.title: Fast-Moving Wildfire Forces Hundreds of Evacuations near Pueblo, Colorado
item.description: The Aspen Acres fire, near Pueblo, Colo., has burned more than 50,000 acres and destroyed hundreds of structures.
item.dc:creator: Maia Spoto
item.media:description: A firefighting plane dropped fire retardant over the Aspen Acres fire on Wednesday in Colorado City, Colo.

item.title: David Hearn, Olympian Canoeist, Is Indicted After Arrest at Trump’s Reflecting Pool
item.description: David Hearn, a former canoeist, is accused of vandalizing the algae-challenged pool, which President Trump has said he would repair and beautify with fresh sealant. But the algae returned and the paint peeled.
item.dc:creator: Campbell Robertson and Alan Feuer
item.media:description: In 2000, David Hearn was one of America’s top competitive canoeists.

item.title: U.S. Officials Believed Israel Was Plotting to Kill Iranian Negotiators
item.description: Any Israeli attempt to kill Abbas Araghchi, the foreign minister, or Mohammad Ghalibaf, the Parliament speaker, would have derailed peace talks, American officials feared.
item.dc:creator: Julian E. Barnes and Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf, left, with Abbas Araghchi, center, traveled to Switzerland in June for a second in-person meeting with Vice President JD Vance and the American delegation.

item.title: Trump-Linked Firm’s Fees for Organizing Freedom 250 Events Remain Secret
item.description: Event Strategies, Inc., a longtime vendor to President Trump’s campaigns, is organizing the Great American State Fair and the July 4 celebrations.
item.dc:creator: David A. Fahrenthold and Andrea Fuller
item.media:description: A company with deep ties to President Trump is organizing the Great American State Fair and the fireworks display planned for Saturday.

item.title: Court Blocks Firings of Intelligence Officers Who Worked on Diversity
item.description: The C.I.A. and Office of the Director of National Intelligence had tried to fire 19 officers at the beginning of the second Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Julian E. Barnes and Seamus Hughes
item.category: Office of the Director of National Intelligence
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: John Ratcliffe, the C.I.A. director, fired employees at the beginning of the second Trump administration who had previously worked on diversity programs.

item.title: In Tumultuous Term, Chief Justice Roberts Took Charge of Unruly Supreme Court
item.description: The chief justice faced down the president, forged unlikely coalitions and achieved long-sought goals.
item.dc:creator: Adam Liptak
item.category: New York Times v Sullivan (Supreme Court Decision)
item.media:description: Chief Justice John Roberts in Washington, D.C., last year.

item.title: Tucker Carlson, Who Broke With Trump, Plans to ‘Help Build a Third Party’
item.description: The influential conservative commentator’s relationship with the president and the G.O.P. fractured over the war with Iran. Now, he says he is charting a new course.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk
item.media:description: Tucker Carlson, a former Trump ally, broke with the G.O.P. over the war with Iran.

item.title: Ocasio-Cortez Endorses Abdul El-Sayed in Crucial Michigan Senate Race
item.description: The endorsement is the first in a contested Senate primary by Representative Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez this year, in a state that Democrats believe they must hold this fall to win a Senate majority.
item.dc:creator: Shane Goldmacher

item.title: California Bans ‘Sell By’ Labels, Hoping to Cut Food Waste
item.description: The law standardizes language around expiration dates, aiming to minimize confusion about when food is safe to eat. More than one-third of food sold nationwide is wasted, the U.S.D.A. says.
item.dc:creator: Yan Zhuang

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Nazaneen Ghaffar, Erin McCann, Christine Hauser and Mark Walker

item.title: Across a Scorching Ohio, Residents Endure Breathtaking Heat
item.description: The heat wave gripping much of the eastern half of the country over the past few days has been especially intense throughout the state.
item.dc:creator: Billy Witz, Sejal Govindarao and Kevin Williams
item.media:credit: Brian Kaiser for The New York Times
item.media:description: Construction workers during a heat wave on Wednesday in Columbus, Ohio.

item.title: Boston Is Drinking Like It’s 1776. The Founding Fathers Would Be Proud.
item.description: A summer of celebratory drinking is underway in the birthplace of the American Revolution. But however tipsy the tourists get, the founders probably had them beat.
item.dc:creator: Jenna Russell
item.media:credit: Veasey Conway for The New York Times
item.media:description: Hundreds of thousands of visitors are expected this summer in Boston, where the American Revolution began, to celebrate the nation’s 250th.

item.title: Republican Senate Candidates Start Their Own Super PACs to Gain Cash Advantage
item.description: Most of the party’s top candidates are starting their own super PACs instead of relying on a powerful group run by Washington leaders. The move allows them to seize control of their financial destinies.
item.dc:creator: Theodore Schleifer
item.media:credit: Nathan Howard/Reuters; Kenny Holston/The New York Times
item.media:description: Representative Ashley Hinson of Iowa and Senator Jon Husted of Ohio are two of the Republican candidates for Senate this year with their own allied super PACs.

item.title: A ‘Muslims Only’ Swim Party and the Backlash That Followed
item.description: A Muslim mother’s plan for an Eid swim party near Dallas drew backlash, then threats that upended her quiet life.
item.dc:creator: J. David Goodman and Desiree Rios
item.media:description: Aminah Knight and her husband, Imam Muhammad Abdullah, pose for a portrait in Fort Worth.
